mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance against zero during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio on 60-day forward returns over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recessionary recovery phase reflects improving capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where industrial production increases by more than 0.5% during the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of stagnant federal funds rates and rising unemployment indicates monetary policy is failing to stimulate labor demand, creating stagflationary fears that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
regime_hint: policy accommodation with weak labor absorption
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns exceed zero in > 50% of periods where funds rates remain flat while unemployment rises, invalidating the negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and dividend payout capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings models adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 workers signals abrupt supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns as investors repriced near-term growth.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances following a payroll decline greater than 50,000, demonstrating the signal lacks predictive validity.

mechanism_narrative: Low core inflation combined with accommodative short-term rates creates a favorable real yield environment that supports equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward 20-day returns in > 55% of observations during disinflationary regimes, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.
